heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 28 May 2026 when 225 were seen.

storm.status: The last storm was on 18 July 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 2nd among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 19 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwave in progress.  So far, 3 heatwaves have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 28 June 2026.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 68.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (202°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 9.1°C) and outside temperature (down 7.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 22%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 27704 lux) and cloud levels (up 2766 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 25286 lux), cloud levels (up 1952 m), and outside humidity (down 9%) when compared to today.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; moderate and mild. Also, dry and less humid conditions and very little to no wind will be present. Possible light showers, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and weak sunlight because of clouds. The weather will likely remain the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
